name: GAL Ops Bank Secure-Message Detection
description: Detects bank encrypted secure-message notifications (secure@bank-example.com) so they can be labelled + surfaced, without confusing them with bank marketing (promo@bank-example.com, an explicit advertising tag). The agent never logs into the bank portal or handles 2FA. Synthetic fixtures; no real data.

input.subject: New secure message (reference 000000000)
input.body: Hello, you have a new message in your secure mailbox. To view it, sign in to the secure site. Messages are sent encrypted.

input.subject: New secure message (reference 111111111)
input.body: You have a new message in your secure mailbox. Messages are sent encrypted to the secure site.

input.subject: How much will your pension be worth? (advertisement)
input.body: New! A Smart service for managing your retirement savings. To unsubscribe from this mailing list, click here.

cases.title: A SaaS invoice is neither secure nor bank marketing
A different bank's email is not an Example Bank secure message

input.subject: Monthly account statement
input.body: Your statement is ready in the app.

cases.title: Retailer using the word 'bank' (+ a 'new message' phrase) is NEITHER

input.subject: Bank-holiday mega deals
input.body: You have a new message about our deals. Come to the store!

cases.title: Bank marketing sender with secure-sounding body is MARKETING, not secure

input.subject: Important update from the bank
input.body: You have a new message in your secure area.
